desc: Katowice is a practical, design-forward Silesian city where post-industrial heritage, major music venues, and excellent transport links create an easy urban base. Demand is steadier than in Kraków, but spring-autumn events and business traffic lift occupancy and flight pressure, while deep winter can bring colder air and quieter tourism streets.
hls: Strong value versus higher-profile Polish city breaks
Excellent regional rail links across Upper Silesia
Useful dual-airport access via KTW and KRK

todo.name: Culture Zone circuit
todo.desc: Pair NOSPR, Spodek, and the Silesian Museum area to see Katowice's industrial-to-modern transformation.

todo.name: Nikiszowiec district walk
todo.desc: Explore the red-brick workers' estate, courtyards, and local galleries tied to Silesian identity.

todo.name: Silesian Museum galleries
todo.desc: Use the underground exhibition spaces for regional history, art, and mining-era context.

todo.name: Mariacka Street evening
todo.desc: Try craft beer bars and contemporary Polish kitchens in the city's liveliest central strip.

todo.name: Park Śląski day
todo.desc: Spend a slower day in one of Europe's largest city parks with zoo, planetarium, and bike paths.

todo.name: Upper Silesian day trip
todo.desc: Use frequent regional trains to nearby Gliwice, Zabrze, or Tychy for industrial heritage sites.

wifi.note: Very dependable around Centrum and business hotels; coworking options are good for short remote-work stays.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Return fares commonly run ~£35–£95 off-peak, ~£95–£165 in shoulder demand, and ~£165+ during school-holiday and major-event periods.

airports.name: Kraków John Paul II International Airport

prac.visa: Poland is in Schengen; UK and US passport holders can generally stay visa-free up to 90 days in any rolling 180-day period.
alerts: Winter inversions can worsen local air quality; check AQI if you are sensitive.
Major concerts/events can spike central-room prices and late-night transport demand.
Business-travel weekdays can tighten hotel availability near the Culture Zone.

sweet: May to September gives the easiest weather window; autumn is still strong for culture-led trips with lower leisure crowding.
